text: Kina maga kaa wa piili wiilire.
translated: The woman is cooking food in the house.

ref: They didn't know anything was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. This is how it will be when the Son of Man appears.
text: Ba wa fun zim di Koosa zi wa tum niu o wa su tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga. Ba ka wa fun saksi di ba siti a siti. N waa nibine be waali ti zi wa chi chen saharɛ.
translated: They went to the farm and Kosa went with them to help them with their work. They did not go to rest, they went to work. The children played under the tree while they worked in the afternoon.

ref: So be on your guard! You don't know when your Lord will come.
text: Ma wa zim a wuzu naa ma kunyana zi wa bur wa. Adaha maa il nɛnɛ.
translated: If you want to go there you must ask for permission. That is the rule.

ref: Those who were wise answered, “There's not enough oil for all of us! Go and buy some for yourselves.”
text: Ninɛɛgɛ ta fun ŋɔ ba, ‘Ya nu ti wa kimak ya maga. Ma la niyogle wati a la yau.’
translated: The woman said to her child, ‘Go and bring my bag. I want to go to the market today.’

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
text: Nar la kpaŋi, waa la zi na a gbaari. Nar ka vɛ kpaŋi, o zi chel a maga niu.
translated: If you speak the truth, your heart will be at peace. If you do not speak the truth, you will always be afraid.

ref: and when I was naked, you gave me clothes to wear. When I was sick, you took care of me, and when I was in jail, you visited me.”
text: Mu wa fun kpak kilaarɛ, ma nyɛ min kilaarɛ mu laari. Wichɔɔrta fun kpak min, ma nyini min. Ba fun tɔ min dɛ, ma wa zaami min.’
translated: If you give me something good, I will also give you something good. If you do something bad to me, I will do the same to you. If you don't do anything to me, I won't do anything to you.

ref: I was hungry, but you did not give me anything to eat, and I was thirsty, but you did not give me anything to drink.
text: Lɔsa funɛ kpak min, ma wa fun nyɛ min diwe mu di. Nyɔksa funɛ kpak min, ma wa fun nyɛ min ni mu nyɔ.
translated: If you give me money, I will give you what you want. If you give me respect, I will give you respect in return.

ref: During the meal Jesus took some bread in his hands. He blessed the bread and broke it. Then he gave it to his disciples and said, “Take this and eat it. This is my body.”
text: Ba funɛ di chene, Yesu fun zer boroboro a sumi Koosa a du a chɔksi a bɔni o gantɛl tiŋsi ta, a ŋɔ ba, “Mu bara nɛɛnɛ. Ma zer a di.”
translated: When it was evening, Jesus took bread and after giving thanks he broke it and gave it to his disciples, saying, “Take and eat. This is my body.”

ref: Jesus took along Peter and the two brothers, James and John. He was very sad and troubled,
text: O funɛ la chene, o fun nyɛ Pita, Jems awa Jɔn, Zɛbɛdi bisa, fun tiŋ un. O fun piili a na namsi kinkan a chi o kaa sou. O tuha fun chɔk kinkan.
translated: He went up the mountain, and he took Peter, James and John, Zebedee's sons, with him. He became changed and his clothes became shining white. He appeared very bright.

ref: The high priest then tore his robe and said, “This man claims to be God! We don't need any more witnesses! You have heard what he said.
text: O funɛ ŋɔ chen, Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta nihɛɛ ti fun chɛsi chɛsi o gar a ŋɔ, “Yɛrɛ ya bukoo kaa ya a dɛu sɛhɛra tima? Ma maga fun a nu chenani o kpa o titi a maksi Koosa.
translated: When he arrived there, Koosa saw some children playing and asked one of them, “Have you seen a man passing by here? I am looking for my father who left before me to come to Koosa.”

ref: But they could not find any, even though many did come and tell lies. At last, two men came forward
text: Ba funɛ nyiniu hali a wa ku. Nidamata fun li ba suwachak a sɛm sɛma a du Yesu ni, amaa ba sɛm ta wa fun ya wuti. Doone nara balɛ fun wa li ba suwachak,
translated: Some people hear but do not understand. Sometimes they hear the word of God about Jesus, but the word does not stay in their hearts. That is why they do not understand, even though they hear the word.

ref: Everyone answered, “We and our own families will take the blame for his death!”
text: Zɛmɛ ta maga fun ŋɔ un, “Koosa la zi na ya wa ya nilulɛ galimsa a lɛrgi ya diŋsa o sou daha.”
translated: "The chief said to them, 'Tomorrow you should go and bring the yams from the farm and put them in the house.'"

ref: There they gave Jesus some wine mixed with a drug to ease the pain. But when Jesus tasted what it was, he refused to drink it.
text: Doone ba fun kpa luri a wa nyɛ Yesu ni a daŋni o nyɔ. O fun lɛmsi un a vɛ nyɔɔli.
translated: There was a man who came to Jesus at night. He asked him about life.

ref: Above his head they put a sign that told why he was nailed there. It read, “This is Jesus, the King of the Jews.”
text: Ba fun wa sɔbi o yaali a ŋɔ, “Yesu nɛɛnɛ. Waa ya Ju tim koora.” Ba fun kpa chenani ba sɔbi chen a mari o nyuha a li daakakli ti ni.
translated: The people asked him and said, “Are you Jesus? Tell us the truth.” He answered them and said that he is not lying to them but is telling them the truth.

ref: went and asked for Jesus' body. Pilate gave orders for it to be given to Joseph,
text: O fun kaali Pailɔt wati a la sumi di ba yisi Yesu a li daakakli ti ni a nyɛ un o la sogli. Pailɔt fun la nɔɔni a tum chen.
translated: Then Pilate called together the chief priests and the rulers and the people, and said to them, "You brought me this man as one who was inciting the people to rebellion."

ref: The guards shook from fear and fell down, as though they were dead.
text: Hansura fun zu lanjimasi ta kinkan. Ba bara funɛ jigiriu a gigmi a chi siwɛ bara.
translated: Hansura went to the market early. Her mother gave her money to buy some things at the market.

ref: Jesus came to them and said: I have been given all authority in heaven and on earth!
text: Yesu fun la dugli ba a ŋɔ, “Koosa funɛ kpa buudaha awa tɛɛnɛ kpok magau a nyɛ min.
translated: Jesus replied to them, "Foxes have holes and birds of the air have nests, but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay his head."

ref: Jesus walked on and soon saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat, mending their nets.
text: O funɛ bu kaali suwachak tama, o fun na niinbisa ba nara balɛ, Jems awa Jɔn, Zɛbɛdi bisa. Ba funɛ nyɛn ba arŋ tuhu ni a kɔɔri ba chamna.
translated: He saw two other brothers, James and John, sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat with their father, preparing their nets.

ref: News about Jesus quickly spread all over Galilee. Jesus Heals Many People (Matthew
text: Lalaknɔ ba naa maga nyɛn Galili pulum ni fun nu chenani o tum. Yesu Fun Tiibi Nidamata (Mat.
translated: After that, Jesus went to the region of Galilee and began to teach there. Jesus Calls His First Disciples (Matt.)

ref: Jesus replied, “We must go to the nearby towns, so that I can tell the good news to those people. This is why I have come.”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “Ma nyɛ ya si a la suwachak tɔ wisa, a naa kaɛɛ, chen ni n zi la ŋɔ ba Koosa wɛu ba nu. Chendaha n fun wa.”
translated: Jesus said to him, “If you want to be perfect, go, sell your possessions and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, follow me.”

ref: That evening after sunset, all who were sick or had demons in them were brought to Jesus.
text: Nha funɛ chel chen, tɔhɔn ti tima kpa ba wichɔɔrsa awa ba naa alizin tagna nyɛn ba ni a wa Yesu wati.
translated: If you believe in your heart, confess with your mouth that Jesus is Lord and you will be saved.

ref: Then so many of them came to the house that there wasn't even standing room left in front of the door. Jesus was still teaching
text: Nara kinkan fun lagmi dooni. Gbampulim wa fun to dɛ ti ni hali dɛ ti zɔɔnɔ ni. Yesu fun ŋɔ ba Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛ.
translated: People should always do good. Everyone should do what is right and avoid what is wrong. Jesus wants us to love one another.

ref: But if you speak against the Holy Spirit, you can never be forgiven. That sin will be held against you forever.
text: Nara vɛ nyɛ Koosa siim weri ti jirima, Koosa wa zi gil chen a nyɛ ba, hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ.”
translated: If you see a Koosa bird sitting on a tree, Koosa will not fly away unless it sees you, even if you throw a stone at it.

ref: Jesus said this because the people were saying that he had an evil spirit in him. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Matthew
text: O funɛ sɛm a nama domi bɔnɛ ŋɔ di alizin tagin kani o nyuhu ni. Yesu Nitiŋɛ Ya O Wuti Walɛ (Mat.
translated: If your right eye makes you sin, take it out and throw it away. Jesus said this (Matt.)

ref: Jesus asked, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
text: Zɛmɛ ba naa funɛ kani a go un, o fun pɛɛsi ba, “Anti ya mu niin wuti awa mu niinbisa?”
translated: When the elders had finished speaking, one of them asked, “Why do you people do things the way you do?”

ref: It is like what happens when a mustard seed is planted in the ground. It is the smallest seed in all the world.
text: O chi kiduha naa ba yir ‘mastad’ ba yaa kpa aa du chene. Waa ya kidusi maga kiwewe.
translated: If you see someone called ‘master’ coming, don’t go near that place. They might punish you for nothing.

ref: So they left the crowd, and his disciples started across the lake with him in the boat. Some other boats followed along.
text: O gantɛl tiŋsi ta fun gil zɛmɛ ta a zu pu Yesu arŋ tuhu ni ba kaali. Bɔnɛ gbanko fun zu arŋ bɔnɛ a tiŋ un.
translated: Some people came to see what Jesus had done and they were amazed. They went back to tell others what they had seen.

ref: He then begged Jesus not to send them away.
text: O funɛ sumi Yesu kinkan di o ta duŋni a a li ba pulum ni.
translated: And then Jesus said that he would not do that again.

ref: But Jesus would not let him. Instead, he said, “Go home to your family and tell them how much the Lord has done for you and how good he has been to you.”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ un, “Aai. Kila i dam walɛ wati a la ŋɔ ba chenani i kunyana suŋi hin a ɔm hin siki.”
translated: Jesus said to him, "No. If you do not let me wash you, you have no part with me."

ref: In the crowd was a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years.
text: Nihaan digi funɛ nyɛn doon a kpak wichɔɔr digi bun fidalɛ. O fun ya nin ka o vɛ peti.
translated: We saw a bird sitting on the big tree. It was there because it was looking for food.

ref: The woman knew what had happened to her. So she came trembling with fear and knelt down in front of Jesus. Then she told him the whole story.
text: Hansura fun kpak haan ti kinkan. O bara fun a jigiri. O fun zim di Yesu tiibi unu a fun wa gbiri o suwachak ni a sɛm o tuha chaani maga a nyɛ un.
translated: The man took the sheep to the river. He gave the sheep to his friend. He said that Jesus wants us to love one another and to help those who are in need.

ref: Jesus did not let anyone go with him except Peter and the two brothers, James and John.
text: Yesu wa fun a daŋni nandigi tiŋ un amaa a nyɛ Pita, Jems awa o niinbe Jɔn ba duŋduŋ funɛ tiŋ un kaali.
translated: Jesus took only Peter, James and his brother John with him.

ref: The girl hurried back and told Herod, “Here and now on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”
text: O fun bur kaali koor ti wati lalaga a la ŋɔ un, “Mu sumi Jɔn nyuhurɛ di ba kpa du pɛŋ ni a wa nyɛ min.”
translated: And then the old man said to them, “Don’t forget what John told you to do when he comes back.”

ref: Jesus replied, “You give them something to eat.” But they asked him, “Don't you know it would take almost a year's wages to buy all of these people something to eat?”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “Ma titi nyɛ ba diwe ba di.” Ba fun pɛɛsi un, “Ei, yɛrɛ ya kaa ya na diwe a nyɛ nara nama ni ba di? Ya yau boroboro sidi tusugsa nyuu, o wa zi tel.”
translated: Jesus answered them, “You give them something to eat.” They said to him, “But, should we go and buy bread worth two hundred silver coins, and give it to them to eat?”

ref: But Jesus said, “The children must first be fed! It isn't right to take away their food and feed it to dogs.”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ haan ti ni, “Nyɛ ba te kpa diwe tima a nyɛ bichɛsi ni ba di puŋ. O wa deŋli wa ba kpa bichɛsi ta diwe a nyɛ vasi ni a di.”
translated: Jesus called them and said, “You know that those who are regarded as rulers of the Gentiles lord it over them, and their high officials exercise authority over them.”

ref: At once the man could hear, and he had no more trouble talking clearly.
text: O diŋsi ta fun vaan puk. O zulum ti burgi. O fun a koti a sɛm sɛma nɛnɛ.
translated: He/she/it went to the farm yesterday. He/she/it worked hard. He/she/it went and returned home in the evening.

ref: The Pharisees came out and started an argument with Jesus. They wanted to test him by asking for a sign from heaven.
text: Farisi tim ta bɔnɛ fun wa Yesu wati a wa di un amɔrɛ a la daŋni o tum alaazibu tuma a biksi ba di ba zim di o kpok ti sir li Koosa watɛɛ.
translated: The Pharisees came and asked Jesus a question to test him, asking if it is lawful for a man to divorce his wife for any reason.

ref: What could you give to get back your soul?
text: Baŋnɛ nibine zi bukoo zer a kpiŋi a na lɛmlɛm titi?
translated: Why did the children not go to school yesterday morning?

ref: Then Elijah and Moses appeared and were talking with Jesus.
text: Ba fun a nyini ka Ɛlaija awa Mosis fun nyɛn Yesu wati a sɛm sɛma.
translated: Then they saw Elijah and Moses talking with Jesus.

ref: Jesus answered: Elijah certainly will come to get everything ready. But don't the Scriptures also say that the Son of Man must suffer terribly and be rejected?
text: O fun ŋɔ ba, “Wutirɛ, Ɛlaija funɛ sir te wau a wa ya kina maga siri. Ba funɛ sɔbiu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni, n waa min nibine be wa wa, ba zi wa zɛɛmi min a namsi min. Yɛrɛ ba fun sɔbi chen?
translated: He said to him, "Truly, Elijah will come first and restore all things. But I tell you, Elijah has already come, and they did not recognize him but did to him whatever they wished. So how is it written about the Son of Man?"

ref: The crowd was really surprised to see Jesus, and everyone hurried over to greet him.
text: Zɛmɛ ta funɛ wa na Yesu, o fun jɛri ba kinkan. Ba fun chɔ a la pu un a zaami.
translated: If you believe in Jesus, he will give you eternal life. He will not let you perish.
